name: MQTT Bridge for Sensor Data Aggregation
description: MQTT bridge system for aggregating and forwarding sensor data to multiple destinations
author: ATHENA Team

baseTopic.title: Base Topic
baseTopic.description: Base MQTT topic for this device

enableRetained.title: Enable Retained Messages
enableRetained.description: Publish MQTT messages with retained flag

metadata.description: Wiring diagram for MQTT bridge with sensors

metadata.content:
# MQTT Bridge for Sensor Data Aggregation

## Overview
This template implements an MQTT bridge system for aggregating sensor data and forwarding it to multiple destinations. It supports multiple sensors, data aggregation, buffering, and real-time configuration.

## Features
- **Multi-Sensor Support**: Configure up to 8 analog sensors
- **Data Aggregation**: Statistical analysis (min, max, average)
- **Offline Buffering**: Store data when MQTT is unavailable
- **Real-time Configuration**: Update settings via MQTT
- **Status Monitoring**: LED indication and status publishing
- **Flexible Topics**: Configurable MQTT topics
- **JSON Payloads**: Structured data format
- **Auto-Reconnect**: Automatic Wi-Fi and MQTT reconnection

## Configuration Options
- **Wi-Fi Settings**: SSID, password for network connection
- **MQTT Settings**: Broker, port, credentials, topics
- **Device ID**: Unique identifier for the bridge
- **Sensor Configuration**: Pin assignments, names, units, multipliers
- **Publish Interval**: Data publishing frequency (1-3600 seconds)
- **Aggregation**: Enable statistical aggregation (default: enabled)
- **Aggregation Window**: Time window for aggregation (10-300 seconds)
- **Buffering**: Enable offline data buffering (default: enabled)
- **Buffer Size**: Maximum buffered readings (10-1000)
- **Status LED**: LED pin for status indication
- **Retained Messages**: Enable MQTT retained flag (default: disabled)

## MQTT Topics
- **Data Topic**: `{baseTopic}/{deviceId}/data`
- **Status Topic**: `{baseTopic}/{deviceId}/status`
- **Config Topic**: `{baseTopic}/{deviceId}/config/+`

## Data Format
### Sensor Data Payload
```json
{
  "deviceId": "athena-bridge-001",
  "timestamp": 1234567890,
  "wifiRSSI": -45,
  "sensors": {
    "temperature": {
      "current": 23.5,
      "average": 23.2,
      "min": 22.8,
      "max": 24.1,
      "count": 10,
      "unit": "°C"
    },
    "humidity": {
      "current": 65.2,
      "average": 64.8,
      "min": 63.5,
      "max": 66.1,
      "count": 10,
      "unit": "%"
    }
  }
}
```

### Status Payload
```json
{
  "deviceId": "athena-bridge-001",
  "status": "online",
  "timestamp": 1234567890,
  "ip": "192.168.1.100",
  "wifiRSSI": -45,
  "sensorCount": 3,
  "bufferCount": 0
}
```

## Configuration Commands
### Update Publish Interval
```json
{
  "interval": 60
}
```
Topic: `{baseTopic}/{deviceId}/config/interval`

### Update Sensor Configuration
```json
{
  "sensors": [
    {"pin": 34, "name": "temperature", "unit": "°C", "multiplier": 0.1},
    {"pin": 35, "name": "humidity", "unit": "%", "multiplier": 0.1}
  ]
}
```
Topic: `{baseTopic}/{deviceId}/config/sensors`

## LED Status Patterns
- **Solid ON**: Connected to MQTT broker
- **Slow Blink**: Connecting to Wi-Fi or MQTT
- **Fast Blink**: Connection error
- **OFF**: Disconnected

## Supported Sensors
- **Analog Sensors**: Any 0-3.3V analog sensor
- **Temperature**: LM35, thermistors, PT100
- **Humidity**: DHT11 (analog mode), capacitive sensors
- **Light**: LDR, photoresistors, BH1750 (analog)
- **Pressure**: MPX5700, pressure sensors
- **Custom**: Any analog sensor with voltage output

## Supported Boards
- ESP32 DevKit v1
- ESP8266 NodeMCU v2

## Dependencies
- WiFi library (built-in)
- PubSubClient library v2.8.0
- ArduinoJson library v6.21.3
- EEPROM library (built-in)

## Setup Instructions
1. Connect sensors to analog pins (GPIO 32-39 on ESP32)
2. Connect status LED to pin {{.ledPin}}
3. Configure Wi-Fi and MQTT settings
4. Upload sketch to device
5. Monitor serial output for status
6. Subscribe to MQTT topics for data

## Advanced Configuration
- **Custom Multipliers**: Calibrate sensor readings
- **Aggregation Windows**: Optimize for sensor response time
- **Buffer Management**: Adjust for network reliability
- **Topic Structure**: Customize for your IoT platform
- **QoS Settings**: Configure message quality of service

## Integration Examples
### Home Assistant
```yaml
sensor:
  - platform: mqtt
    name: "Temperature"
    state_topic: "athena/bridge/athena-bridge-001/data"
    value_template: "{{value_json.sensors.temperature.current}}"
    unit_of_measurement: "°C"
```

### Node-RED
```javascript
msg.topic = "athena/bridge/athena-bridge-001/config/interval";
msg.payload = JSON.stringify({interval: 30});
return msg;
```

### AWS IoT Core
- Use device ID as client ID
- Configure TLS certificates
- Map topics to AWS IoT topics

## Performance Specifications
- **Sensor Reading**: <10ms per sensor
- **Aggregation Update**: Configurable window
- **MQTT Publish**: <100ms typical
- **Memory Usage**: ~30KB for buffers
- **Power Consumption**: ~100mA (Wi-Fi active)
- **Data Rate**: Configurable (1-3600s intervals)

## Troubleshooting
- **Wi-Fi Connection**: Check SSID/password and signal strength
- **MQTT Connection**: Verify broker address and credentials
- **Sensor Readings**: Check analog connections and power
- **Data Not Publishing**: Verify MQTT topic subscriptions
- **Buffer Overflow**: Increase buffer size or reduce frequency
- **LED Not Working**: Check LED connections and pin assignment

## Security Considerations
- **MQTT Authentication**: Use username/password
- **TLS Encryption**: Enable MQTT over TLS
- **Network Security**: Use WPA2/WPA3 networks
- **Access Control**: Limit MQTT topic permissions
- **Data Privacy**: Avoid sensitive data in topics

## Use Cases
- **Industrial Monitoring**: Factory sensor aggregation
- **Smart Agriculture**: Environmental monitoring
- **Building Management**: HVAC and lighting control
- **Weather Stations**: Meteorological data collection
- **IoT Gateways**: Protocol conversion and buffering
